node.statement: (Pointedness forces exit from cone) Since V^+ is pointed, we cannot have both D ∈ V^+ and -D ∈ V^+ (this would imply D = 0). Since x lies in the relative interior of F and D ∈ span(F), for sufficiently small |t| we have x + tD ∈ F ⊆ V^+. But the line cannot remain in V^+ in both directions indefinitely. Define t^+ = sup{t > 0 : x + tD ∈ V^+} and t^- = sup{t > 0 : x - tD ∈ V^+}. At least one of t^+, t^- is finite.
